name: Goblet Squat
desc: Hold a DB at chest height. Sit down between your heels, elbows inside knees at the bottom. Drive through the full foot to stand.

name: Tempo Goblet Squat
desc: 3 seconds down, 1 second pause at the bottom, drive up. The tempo is the work — don't rush it. Builds serious quad strength.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
desc: Rear foot elevated on bench. Front shin stays vertical. Lower slowly — 3 seconds down. Builds serious single-leg strength.

name: Romanian Deadlift
desc: Soft knee, hinge at the hip. Feel the hamstring stretch before driving hips forward. Don't round the back chasing depth.

name: Single-leg RDL
desc: Balance on one leg, hinge until deep hamstring pull. Both hands on DBs. Control is everything here.

name: Hip Thrust
desc: Upper back on bench, DB or heavy bag across hips. Drive through heels, full glute squeeze at the top. Hold 1 second.

name: Side Step Lunge
desc: Big lateral step, push hips back as you sink. Keep the stepping foot fully flat. Return to center before repeating.

name: Curtsy Reverse Lunge
desc: Step back and cross behind the standing leg. Targets glute med hard. Keep hips square and torso tall.

name: Front Foot Elevated Split Squat
desc: Elevate front foot on a plate or step. Increases range of motion and quad stretch. Keep torso upright.

name: Medicine Ball Lunge
desc: Hold med ball at chest. Step forward, lower back knee toward floor. Drive through front heel to return.

name: Sumo Squat
desc: Wide stance, toes turned out 45°. Sit straight down between your heels. Targets inner thighs differently than a goblet squat.

name: Glute Bridge
desc: Feet flat, drive through heels. Squeeze glutes hard at the top and hold 1 second. Load with DB or heavy bag across hips.

name: Calf Raise
desc: Single leg for more challenge. Full range — pause at the bottom, drive up. Use a step edge if available.

name: Resistance Band Hip Side Step
desc: Band around ankles. Stay in a slight squat throughout. Don't let feet come together — keep tension in the band the whole time.

name: Lunge Jump (Split Squat Jump)
desc: Start in a lunge, explode up and switch legs in the air, land softly in the opposite lunge. Plyometric — absorb the landing with control.

name: DB Bench Press
desc: Full ROM — touch chest, lock out at top. Control the descent 2 seconds down. Flat or incline depending on the day.

name: DB Incline Press
desc: Bench at ~30°. Keep elbows at 45° angle from torso. Targets the upper chest more than flat.

name: Seated DB Overhead Press
desc: Seated on bench, core braced. Press to full lockout, lower controlled. Don't flare ribs at the top.

name: Half-Kneeling Single-arm DB Press (Landmine style)
desc: Kneeling on one knee, press a single DB overhead at a slight angle. Core works hard to resist rotation. Similar pattern to a landmine press without a barbell.

name: DB Lateral Raise
desc: Lead with elbows, slight forward lean. Raise to shoulder height. Slow on the way down — 3 seconds. Lighter than you think.

name: DB Shoulder Shrug
desc: Heavy DBs, shrug straight up toward ears. Hold 1 second at the top. Don't roll the shoulders — straight up and down.

name: Single-arm DB Row
desc: Brace on bench. Let shoulder blade fully protract at the bottom, retract hard at the top. No torso rotation.

name: DB Rear Delt Fly
desc: Face down on incline bench. Lead with elbows, squeeze shoulder blades together at the top.

name: DB Chest Fly
desc: Slight bend in elbows throughout. Feel the stretch at the bottom, squeeze at the top. 20 lb DBs.

name: DB Skull Crusher
desc: Upper arms vertical, lower to temples. Slow and controlled. Keep elbows from flaring wide.

name: Incline DB Curl
desc: Arms hanging on incline bench — maximizes the bicep stretch at the bottom. No swinging.

name: DB Hammer Curl
desc: Neutral grip. Slow on the way down — 3 seconds. Hits brachialis for arm thickness regular curls miss.

name: Push-up
desc: Body in a straight line, elbows at 45° from torso. Rotate variations: wide grip, close grip, decline, archer.

name: Diamond Push-up
desc: Hands together under chest forming a diamond shape. Elbows track back, not out. Serious tricep builder — harder than it looks.

name: Plyometric Push-up from Bench
desc: Hands on bench, lower to the bench then explode up so hands leave the surface. Land soft and controlled. Builds upper body power.

name: Resistance Band Pull-apart
desc: Arms straight, pull to full stretch. Slow controlled return. Great for posture and shoulder health.

name: Resistance Band Pulldown
desc: Anchor band overhead. Drive elbows down and back. Squeeze lats at the bottom of each rep.

name: Resistance Band Archer
desc: Anchor band at chest height. Hold with both hands extended, then pull one elbow back like drawing a bow. Rotate through the torso slightly. Great for scapular strength.

name: Farmer Carry
desc: Heaviest DBs available. Stand tall, shoulders packed back and down. Don't let the weight pull you sideways.

name: Plate Circles
desc: 25 lb plate, arms extended. Draw big slow circles at shoulder height. Shoulder stability and core endurance work.

name: Half-Kneeling Clean and Press
desc: Kneel on one knee, inside arm curls the DB up (clean), presses overhead, hammer curls back down, sets it down. Anti-rotation through the whole movement. You invented a good one.

name: Bird-dog
desc: On all fours. Opposite arm and leg, extend slowly. Hold 2–3 seconds. Hips stay level — don't rotate.

name: Dead Bug
desc: Lie on back, arms up, knees at 90°. Lower opposite arm and leg without letting your lower back lift. Exhale on the way down.

name: Boat Pose
desc: Seated, lean back, legs lifted, arms extended forward. Keep chest tall. Breathe steadily — don't hold your breath.

name: Plank
desc: Forearms or hands, body in a straight line. Brace everything. Don't let hips sag or pike up. 20–45 second holds.

name: Side Plank
desc: On forearm or hand, body in a straight line side-on. Stack feet or stagger. Hold steady — don't let hips drop.

name: Resistance Band Anti-rotation Hold
desc: Band anchored at chest height. Arms extended straight out, resist the band pulling you into rotation. Core braced the whole time.

name: Trunk Rotation with Resistance Band
desc: Band anchored at side. Hold with both hands extended, rotate away from anchor through the torso. Control the return — don't let the band pull you back. Opposite movement to the anti-rotation hold.

name: Med Ball Slam
desc: Full extension overhead, violent exhale on the slam. Hinge to pick it up — don't round your back on the retrieve.

name: Med Ball Wall Throw
desc: Stand perpendicular to wall. Load through hips and core, throw from rotation. Generate power, catch and control the rebound.

name: Clam Shell
desc: Side lying, hips stacked. Open top knee like a clam, hold 1 second. Resistance band above knees adds real challenge.

name: Clam Shell Side Plank
desc: Side plank position, then open top knee against a resistance band. Hips stay stacked and high. Glute med and obliques together.

name: Jumping Jacks
desc: Classic warm-up. Get blood moving and joints warm before loading anything. Easy pace — prep work, not the main event.

name: Inchworm
desc: From standing, hinge and walk hands out to a plank. Pause, walk feet to hands. Builds hamstring mobility and shoulder stability.

name: Resistance Band Jabs
desc: Band anchored behind you, punch forward alternating arms. Keep core tight. Good warm-up or cardio finisher.

name: DB Curl to Press
desc: Curl up to shoulder height, then press overhead. Two movements in one. Great for time efficiency and shoulder stability.

name: Vinyasa Flow
desc: Down dog to cobra to up dog to child's pose. Move with your breath — inhale to extend, exhale to fold. Recovery and mobility.

name: Cat-Cow
desc: On all fours, alternate between arching and rounding the spine. Breathe into each position. Warms up the spine gently.

name: Hip Rotation Stretch
desc: Figure-four on your back, or standing hip circles. Breathe into each rep. Let the hips open up after lower body loading.
